title: Arrondissement of Niort

infobox.value: is an [arrondissement](/wiki/Arrondissements_of_France "Arrondissements of France") of France in the [Deux-Sèvres](/wiki/Deux-S%C3%A8vres "Deux-Sèvres") [department](/wiki/Departments_of_France "Departments of France") in the [Nouvelle-Aquitaine](/wiki/Nouvelle-Aquitaine "Nouvelle-Aquitaine") [region](/wiki/Regions_of_France "Regions of France"). It has 117 [communes](/wiki/Communes_of_France "Communes of France").[[2]](#cite_note-insee2-2) Its population is 199,565 (2021), and its area is 2,445.1 km2 (944.1 sq mi).[[3]](#cite_note-insee-3)
["Populations de référence 2023"](https://www.insee.fr/fr/statistiques/8681011) (in French). National Institute of Statistics and Economic Studies. 18 December 2025.
["Comparateur de territoire, géographie au 01/01/2024"](https://www.insee.fr/fr/statistiques/1405599?geo=ARR-791+ARR-792+ARR-793). [INSEE](/wiki/INSEE "INSEE"). Retrieved 11 December 2024.

The **Arrondissement of Niort** is an [arrondissement](/wiki/Arrondissements_of_France "Arrondissements of France") of France in the [Deux-Sèvres](/wiki/Deux-S%C3%A8vres "Deux-Sèvres") [department](/wiki/Departments_of_France "Departments of France") in the [Nouvelle-Aquitaine](/wiki/Nouvelle-Aquitaine "Nouvelle-Aquitaine") [region](/wiki/Regions_of_France "Regions of France"). It has 117 [communes](/wiki/Communes_of_France "Communes of France").[[2]](#cite_note-insee2-2) Its population is 199,565 (2021), and its area is 2,445.1 km2 (944.1 sq mi).[[3]](#cite_note-insee-3)

## Composition

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arrondissement_of_Niort&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

The arrondissement of Niort was created in 1800.[[4]](#cite_note-4) At the January 2018 reorganisation of the arrondissements of Deux-Sèvres, it lost 21 communes to the [arrondissement of Parthenay](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Parthenay "Arrondissement of Parthenay").[[5]](#cite_note-arrete-5)

As a result of the reorganisation of the [cantons of France](/wiki/Cantons_of_France "Cantons of France") which came into effect in 2015, the borders of the cantons are no longer related to the borders of the arrondissements. The cantons of the arrondissement of Niort were, as of January 2015:[[6]](#cite_note-insee2012-6)

1. Beauvoir-sur-Niort
2. Brioux-sur-Boutonne
3. [Celles-sur-Belle](/wiki/Canton_of_Celles-sur-Belle "Canton of Celles-sur-Belle")
4. Champdeniers-Saint-Denis
5. Chef-Boutonne
6. Coulonges-sur-l'Autize
7. [Frontenay-Rohan-Rohan](/wiki/Canton_of_Frontenay-Rohan-Rohan "Canton of Frontenay-Rohan-Rohan")
8. Lezay
9. Mauzé-sur-le-Mignon
10. [Melle](/wiki/Canton_of_Melle "Canton of Melle")
11. La Mothe-Saint-Héray
12. Niort-Est
13. Niort-Nord
14. Niort-Ouest
15. Prahecq
16. Saint-Maixent-l'École-1
17. Saint-Maixent-l'École-2
18. Sauzé-Vaussais
